description: This JSON defines the full data contract between the frontend (Next.js) and the backend (AI logic generator). The frontend sends a plain-English automation instruction, and the backend must return a structured response matching this schema exactly. This file also serves as the reference guide for all supported ladder logic block types, instruction list syntax, and example scenarios.

api_contract.description: Accepts a natural language automation instruction and returns ladder logic blocks, an AI explanation, and a Mitsubishi-compatible instruction list.

input.description: Plain-English description of the desired automation logic.
input.example: Start motor when X0 is pressed, stop when X1 is pressed, and X2 acts as emergency stop.

ladder.description: Ordered list of ladder logic blocks forming a single rung. The frontend renders these left to right. Contacts come first, coil comes last.

explanation.description: Human-readable AI explanation of what the ladder rung does. Rendered in the right-side Explanation Panel. Use newlines (\n) and bullet points for readability.
explanation.example:
Based on the input logic description:
- X0 acts as the start contact (normally open).
- X2 acts as an emergency stop (normally closed).
- Y0 is the output coil representing the activated motor.

⚠️ Ensure Emergency Stop is also hardwired for safety redundancy.

instructionList.description: Mitsubishi GX Works-compatible instruction list. Each instruction on its own line. Rendered in the Output Console. User can copy or export this directly to program their PLC.
instructionList.example:
LD X0
ANDN X2
OUT Y0

ladder_block_schema.description: Represents a single element (contact or coil) on a ladder rung.

type.description: The type of ladder block. See ladder_block_types for full details on each.

label.description: The PLC address label for this block. For Mitsubishi: inputs are X0-XFF, outputs are Y0-YFF, internal relays are M0-M9999, timers are T0-T255.

contact.name: Normally Open Contact (NO)
contact.description: Passes current when the associated bit is ON (1). Think of it as a push button that is open by default and closes when activated.
use_cases: Interlock conditions that must be TRUE to proceed

example.label: X0
example.meaning: X0 input must be ON for current to flow through this contact

contact_nc.name: Normally Closed Contact (NC)
contact_nc.description: Passes current when the associated bit is OFF (0). Think of it as a safety guard that is closed by default and opens when activated. Critical for emergency stops and safety interlocks.
use_cases: Emergency stop buttons (must be closed for machine to run)
Safety gate or door interlock switches
Fault conditions that block operation when true

example.label: X2
example.meaning: X2 input must be OFF for current to flow — if X2 activates (e.g. E-Stop pressed), current is blocked and output turns off

contact_nc.safety_note: ⚠️ Emergency stops should always be NC contacts in ladder logic AND hardwired in the physical panel for redundant safety.

coil.name: Output Coil
coil.description: The output of a rung. Energizes (turns ON) when all conditions to its left allow current to flow. Represents a physical output (motor, valve, lamp) or an internal relay.
use_cases: Internal relay flags (M coils) for complex logic

example.label: Y0
example.meaning: Y0 output turns ON when all preceding contacts allow current to flow through the rung

important_rules: Only one coil per rung (for simple logic). Use SET/RST for latching.
Coil must always be the LAST element in the ladder block array.
Do not use the same output coil (Y address) in more than one rung — this causes double-coil issues.

mitsubishi_address_reference.description: Standard Mitsubishi FX/Q-series PLC address ranges used for block labels.

X.name: Input Relay
X.description: Physical input points. Connected to sensors, buttons, switches.

Y.name: Output Relay
Y.description: Physical output points. Connected to motors, valves, lamps.

M.name: Internal Relay (Auxiliary Relay)
M.description: Software-only relay bits. Used for intermediate logic, flags, and latching conditions. Not physically connected.

T.name: Timer
T.description: On-delay timers. T0–T199 are 100ms resolution, T200–T255 are 10ms resolution.

rung_construction_rules.description: Rules the backend must follow when constructing the ladder array. The frontend renders the array as a single horizontal rung from left to right.
rules: The ladder array must have at least 2 elements: one or more contacts, and exactly one coil.
Contacts must appear BEFORE the coil. The coil is always the last element.
For serial (AND) logic: add multiple contacts in sequence before the coil.
For parallel (OR) logic: this single-rung schema does not support branching visually — keep to series logic per rung. Complex parallel logic should use multiple rungs with M (internal relay) coils.
Labels must use valid Mitsubishi addresses (X, Y, M, T, C prefix).
Do not repeat the same output label (Y or M) across multiple rungs unless using SET/RST pattern.

instruction_list_syntax_guide.description: How to construct the instructionList string. It must be Mitsubishi GX Works-compatible mnemonic code.
instruction_list_syntax_guide.format: One instruction per line, uppercase, address immediately after instruction with a space.

common_instructions.LD: Load — starts a new rung with a NO contact. Usage: LD X0
common_instructions.LDI: Load Inverse — starts a new rung with a NC contact. Usage: LDI X2
common_instructions.AND: AND — serial NO contact. Usage: AND X1
common_instructions.ANI: AND Inverse — serial NC contact. Usage: ANI X2
common_instructions.OR: OR — parallel NO contact. Usage: OR M0
common_instructions.ORI: OR Inverse — parallel NC contact. Usage: ORI M1
common_instructions.OUT: Output — energizes a coil. Usage: OUT Y0
common_instructions.SET: Latch ON — keeps coil ON even after trigger released. Usage: SET M0
common_instructions.RST: Reset — turns OFF a latched coil. Usage: RST M0
common_instructions.END: End of program. Always the last instruction.

instruction_list_syntax_guide.example_complete_program:
LD X0
ANI X2
OUT Y0
END

example_scenarios.input: Start motor when X0 is pressed. Stop when X1 is pressed. X2 is emergency stop.

expected_response.explanation:
Basic motor start/stop with emergency stop:
- X0 (NO) is the Start pushbutton.
- X1 (NC) is the Stop pushbutton — breaks the circuit when pressed.
- X2 (NC) is the Emergency Stop — highest priority safety interlock.
- Y0 is the motor output contactor.

⚠️ Add a Y0 seal-in contact in parallel with X0 for self-latching (memory) behavior.
expected_response.instructionList:
LD X0
ANI X1
ANI X2
OUT Y0
END

example_scenarios.input: Turn ON conveyor Y1 when proximity sensor X3 detects part, but only if safety gate X4 is closed.

expected_response.explanation:
Conveyor activation with gate interlock:
- X3 (NO) is the proximity sensor — must detect a part to allow output.
- X4 (NC) is the safety gate switch — must be closed (OFF signal) for operation. If gate opens, X4 energizes and breaks the rung.
- Y1 is the conveyor drive output.
expected_response.instructionList:
LD X3
ANI X4
OUT Y1
END

example_scenarios.input: Turn on indicator lamp Y0 when switch X0 is ON.

expected_response.explanation:
Simple on/off control:
- X0 (NO) is the control switch.
- Y0 is the indicator lamp output.

When X0 turns ON, Y0 energizes immediately.

frontend_rendering_notes.description: How the frontend consumes this response — useful for the backend to understand visual output.
frontend_rendering_notes.ladder_render: The frontend (LadderPreview.tsx) renders all ladder[] blocks as a single horizontal rung on screen. contact = -| |-, contact_nc = -|/|-, coil = -( )-. The rung wire connects them left to right.
frontend_rendering_notes.explanation_render: The explanation string is rendered with whitespace: pre-wrap in the right panel. Use \n for line breaks and bullet points starting with - for formatting.
frontend_rendering_notes.instructionList_render: Rendered in a monospace console at the bottom (OutputConsole.tsx). User can copy to clipboard or download as a .txt file for GX Works import.
frontend_rendering_notes.state_management: All three fields (ladder, explanation, instructionList) are stored in Zustand (plcStore.ts) and remain until the user clicks Clear or generates new logic.
